name: Zero Width Space (ZWSP)
description: The most common invisible character. Used to bypass word filters, create blank names in ames, or control line breaks.

name: Braille Pattern Blank
description: An empty space character from the Braille alphabet. It acts as a solid space and is widely used for creating blank usernames in games like Among Us, PUBG, and Free Fire.

name: Zero Width Non-Joiner (ZWNJ)
description: Used to place two characters next to each other without forming a ligature.

name: Zero Width Joiner (ZWJ)
description: Used to combine multiple emoji into a single composite emoji, or to force ligatures.

name: Non-Breaking Space (NBSP)
description: A standard space that prevents an automatic line break at its position. Useful in HTML.

name: Left-To-Right Override
description: Forces the following characters to be treated as left-to-right text.

name: Right-To-Left Override
description: Forces the following characters to be treated as right-to-left text. It actually reverses the text visually!

name: Hangul Filler
description: A popular empty character from the Korean alphabet. Used heavily in mobile games and Discord formatting to create large blank gaps.

name: Mongolian Vowel Separator
description: Originally an invisible character used in Mongolian text. Note: It may render as a tofu block on some very old systems.

name: En Quad
description: A space equal in width to exactly one 'n' / 1/2 of an em.

name: Em Quad
description: A space equal to the full point size of a font (an 'm' width).

name: En Space
description: Equivalent to an En Quad.

name: Em Space
description: Equivalent to an Em Quad.

name: Three-Per-Em Space
description: A space 1/3 of an em wide.

name: Four-Per-Em Space
description: A space 1/4 of an em wide.

name: Six-Per-Em Space
description: A space 1/6 of an em wide.

name: Figure Space
description: A space equal to the width of a digit in fonts with fixed-width numerals.

name: Punctuation Space
description: A space equal to the width of a narrow punctuation mark (like a period or comma).

name: Thin Space
description: A narrow space, often used inside nested quotes or around dashes.

name: Hair Space
description: The narrowest standard space available.

name: Word Joiner
description: Prevents line-breaks where they would otherwise occur.

name: Zero Width No-Break Space
description: Also known as a Byte Order Mark (BOM). Used rarely as a zero-width space.
